title: TVL Module
description: Schema for Tuned Variables Language (TVL) modules. Captures the normative surface syntax for validation tooling.

tvl.description: Module metadata block (`tvl.module`) identifying the namespace of the specification; see Listing 9.

module.description: Fully-qualified module identifier (e.g., `corp.product.spec`), matching the paper's namespace convention.

validation.description: Optional validation configuration for tooling (e.g., skip certain checks during development).

skip_budget_checks.description: When true, skip validation of exploration budget constraints.

skip_cost_estimation.description: When true, skip cost estimation checks for derived constraints.

environment.description: Environment snapshot supplying deployment bindings and numeric context used to specialise domains and operational preconditions.

snapshot_id.description: RFC3339 timestamp that labels the environment snapshot (`E_τ` in §III).

bindings.description: Opaque deployment references pinned for reproducibility and auditability (for example retriever index IDs, gateway regions, or catalog versions). Bindings are not numeric operational-precondition symbols.

context.description: Optional numeric environment context used by operational preconditions under `constraints.derived`. Operational arithmetic must reference `env.context.*`.

evaluation_set.description: Evaluation set definition that anchors the dataset and optional randomness seed used in validation runs (§III.B).

dataset.description: Canonical identifier or URI for the evaluation dataset (Listing 9 `evaluation_set.dataset`).

seed.description: Optional deterministic seed for stochastic evaluation sets.

tvars.description: Ordered list of TVAR declarations (Γ) including `name`, `type`, and `domain` entries.

constraints.description: Constraint sets split between structural (TVAR formulas) and derived (linear constraints over numeric `env.context.*` symbols) clauses.

structural.description: Typed DNF clauses over TVARs that must hold for structural feasibility.

when.description: Antecedent formula (optional) specifying the guard for an implication.

then.description: Consequent formula enforced when the `when` guard is satisfied.

expr.description: Standalone structural formula without implication sugar.

derived.description: Linear real-valued predicates over numeric `env.context.*` symbols used for operational validation.

require.description: Linear inequality or equality (affine form) that must hold under the environment snapshot. Operational preconditions may reference only numeric `env.context.*` symbols.

objectives.description: Primary and banded objectives evaluated by the promotion gate (maximize/minimize or TOST band).

promotion_policy.description: Promotion gate configuration controlling ε-Pareto dominance, error tolerances, and optional chance constraints (§III.G).

dominance.description: Dominance relation applied to objective vectors; TVL 1.0 mandates ε-Pareto.

alpha.description: Family-wise error rate budget (0 < α < 1); defaults to 0.05 when omitted (Listing 10).

min_effect.description: Per-objective ε tolerances that define acceptable deltas for dominance comparisons.

additionalProperties.description: Non-negative ε applied to the named objective.

adjust.description: Multiple-testing adjustment strategy applied before promotion. Options: none (no adjustment), bonferroni (FWER control), holm (FWER control, more powerful), BH (Benjamini–Hochberg FDR control).

chance_constraints.description: Optional hard constraints expressed as minimum confidence thresholds on metrics.

name.description: Metric identifier constrained at promotion time.

threshold.description: Target value the metric must satisfy.

confidence.description: Confidence level for the chance constraint evaluation.

tie_breakers.description: Secondary ordering rules for resolving ties after ε-Pareto comparison.

additionalProperties.description: Tie-breaking strategy for the corresponding objective.

exploration.description: Exploration plan describing how the tuner explores the search space (strategy, sampling, budgets) as in Listing 5.

strategy.description: Primary search strategy configuration (grid, TPE, CMA-ES, NSGA-II, etc.).

type.description: Name of the exploration algorithm to run.

initial_sampling.description: Optional design for seeding the search (e.g., `latin_hypercube`).

parallelism.description: Settings related to concurrent trial execution.

max_parallel_trials.description: Upper bound on concurrently scheduled trials.

convergence.description: Optional stopping rule based on convergence metrics (e.g., hypervolume improvement).

window.description: Sliding window size for convergence metric aggregation.

threshold.description: Minimum improvement required to continue exploration.

budgets.description: Hard limits on trials, cost, or wall-clock runtime used during exploration (§III.H).

max_spend_usd.description: Budget cap on spend in USD; optional when cost estimation is available.

max_wallclock_s.description: Maximum wall-clock duration in seconds allowed for the exploration run.

TVarDecl.description: Single tuned-variable declaration (`(name, type, domain)` in Listing 8).

name.description: Unique TVAR identifier; dotted names allowed for nested structures (§III.A).

type.description: Declared TVAR type. TVL 1.0 supports primitive, enum, tuple, and `callable[ProtoId]` forms.

domain.description: Domain specification applicable to the declared type; specialised via the environment (§III.A, Appendix X).

DomainSpec.description: Union type covering the admissible domain encodings described in Listing 9.

EnumVals.description: Finite set of literal values (used for enums or small discrete spaces).

range.description: Closed numeric interval `[min, max]` (inclusive bounds) from Appendix X.

resolution.description: Optional discretisation step for continuous ranges.

set.description: Explicit enumerated set encoded as an object wrapper (handy for richer metadata).

SetVals.description: Wrapper form for explicit discrete sets when additional annotations are needed.

registry.description: Lookup token for registry-backed domains (e.g., callable protocol IDs). Resolved lazily at specialization time.

filter.description: Optional implementation-defined predicate for filtering registry entries (e.g., 'version >= 2.0').

version.description: Optional version constraint for the registry lookup (e.g., '>=1.0.0', '~2.3').

RegistryRef.description: Domain referencing an external registry of admissible values. Resolution: registry_ref → resolve(E_τ, R) → set[values]

components.description: Tuple domain as a list of component domains. Semantics: Cartesian product of components.

TupleComponents.description: Tuple domain specification encoded as component domains (product semantics).

TupleComponentDomainSpec.description: Domain forms allowed inside tuple components. Float ranges in tuple components require an explicit resolution.

resolution.description: Grid step used to enumerate tuple-component float ranges.

TupleRange.description: Range domain for tuple components: integer ranges can omit resolution; non-integer ranges require it.

metric_ref.description: Optional stable metric identifier resolved by the evaluation harness (for example `metrics.latency_p95_ms.v1`).
Optional stable metric identifier resolved by the evaluation harness for the banded objective.

oneOf.description: Closed interval [L, U] representing acceptable band bounds.

center.description: Mean value around which the tolerance band is enforced.

target.description: Target region for the banded objective (interval form or center/tolerance).

test.description: Statistical test used for band validation; TVL 1.0 mandates TOST.

alpha.description: Significance level for the band test.

Objective.description: Objective definition supporting both scalar dominance and banded (TOST) objectives, with an optional declarative metric reference.
